nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact branded entry for Dayao Orange Promise exists in USDA FoodData Central or HPB Foodo. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy 'Orange drink, canned' (closest approved-source analogue for a sweetened orange-flavoured beverage) and cross-referenced with the input record values (energy 40 kcal/100g, carbohydrate 10g/100g, sugar 9.33g/100g, sodium 5mg/100g, protein 0.17g/100g, fat 0g/100g, dietary fibre 0.17g/100g, vitamin C 10mg/100g). Input values are consistent with a diluted sweetened orange drink and are retained with medium confidence. Fat is 0g by product formulation (confirmed zero for this beverage category per USDA SR Legacy). Protein is trace (0.17g/100g) consistent with orange drink category.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. — Orange juice, unsweetened (GI 50); sweetened orange drink estimated higher at GI 66 consistent with University of Sydney GI Database entry for orange cordial/drink.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Dayao Orange Promise. GI of 66 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for sweetened orange drink/cordial (GI ~65–68), consistent with the peer-reviewed Atkinson et al. 2008 international GI tables. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (66 × 30.0) / 100 = 19.8, using 300g serving with 10g carbohydrate/100g = 30g carbohydrate per serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 9.33g sugar/100g; added sugar independently raises blood pressure via insulin-mediated sodium retention regardless of low sodium content (5mg/100g)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this sugar-sweetened beverage, as its high added-sugar content (9.33g/100g) independently elevates blood pressure risk through insulin-mediated sodium retention and sympathetic nervous system activation, per AHA 2018 dietary guidelines; choose plain water or unsweetened beverages instead.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with GI 66 and 9.33g sugar/100g; liquid sugar from orange concentrate absorbs rapidly causing postprandial glucose spikes; negligible dietary fibre (0.17g/100g) provides no buffering
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this sugar-sweetened orange drink entirely, as its high sugar content (9.33g/100g), medium-to-high GI (66), and negligible fibre (0.17g/100g) cause rapid postprandial glucose elevation; whole oranges with intact fibre are a safer alternative.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High added-sugar content (9.33g/100g) in a sugar-sweetened beverage promotes hypertriglyceridaemia and endothelial inflammation; fat and saturated fat are zero, which is neutral for CVD lipid risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit or avoid this sugar-sweetened beverage, as regular consumption of added sugar at this level (9.33g/100g) is associated with elevated triglycerides, reduced HDL cholesterol, and increased cardiovascular risk per AHA 2016 added-sugar guidelines; the absence of fat and saturated fat is the only neutral factor.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Added sugar promotes pro-inflammatory mediators; vitamin C content (10mg/100g) from concentrate is insufficient to confer meaningful anti-inflammatory benefit
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this sugar-sweetened beverage, as its added sugar content promotes systemic inflammation and the small vitamin C contribution from orange concentrate is insufficient to offset this pro-inflammatory effect; whole fresh oranges or other whole fruits are preferable.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Dayao Orange Promise is a sweetened orange-flavoured beverage sold in Singapore, marketed under the Dayao brand and available through food delivery platforms such as GrabFood. It is classified as an ultra-processed fruit juice drink made from water, orange concentrate, added sugar, citric acid, and vitamin C (ascorbic acid). The drink is mixed and served chilled, typically in a standard cup or bottle of approximately 300g. Per 100g, it provides 40 kcal, 10g of carbohydrates (of which 9.33g is sugar), 0.17g of protein, 0g of fat, 0.17g of dietary fibre, 5mg of sodium, and 10mg of vitamin C. The glycemic index is estimated at 66 (medium), yielding a glycemic load of approximately 19.8 per 300g serving, making it a significant source of rapidly absorbed liquid sugar. Clinically, this beverage is a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) and is unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis due to its high added-sugar content and negligible fibre. It is vegetarian, vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free. The Dayao brand is associated with Chinese-style beverages popular in Singapore and Southeast Asia. Despite the 'Noodles' cuisine tag in the source platform, this product is correctly classified as a Beverage.

confidence_reason: No exact branded entry for Dayao Orange Promise exists in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB Foodo. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy 'Orange drink, canned' as the closest approved-source analogue and are consistent with the input record values (provenance: ai_generated, confidence 0.7). GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for sweetened orange drinks. Medium confidence is appropriate given the absence of a direct approved-source match.
notes: fat_g is confirmed zero by product formulation — this is a water-based sweetened orange-flavoured beverage with no fat-containing ingredients; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy 'Orange drink, canned'. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to 0.0 consistent with zero total fat. trans_fat_g is 0.0 confirmed per product category. potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed micronutrient and moisture data for this specific branded sweetened orange drink are not available in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB Foodo as of 2024; the input record did not supply these values. The 'Noodles' cuisine tag in the source GrabFood listing is a miscategorisation; this product is correctly classified as a Beverage. Dayao is a Chinese beverage brand; primary cuisine is classified as Chinese with Singapore as country of origin. GI of 66 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database for sweetened orange drink/cordial; no direct GI measurement for this branded product was found in approved sources.
